evidence_quotes.quote: publisher shutdowns of online-connected games, even when games still have an active player base.

paragraphs.text: I appreciate the European Commissions initiative through the Digital Fairness Act to address unfair commercial practices such as dark patterns, misleading influencer marketing, addictive design, and unfair personalisation. As a long-time gamer, Id like to highlight one area that feels overlooked: publisher shutdowns of online-connected games, even when games still have an active player base. In many cases, titles like The Crew have been removed, and their servers completely switched offmeaning even legitimate buyers lose access, including to offline progress or achievements. This erases player investment entirely. One issue less obvious to nongamers is the effect on trophy or achievement systemsprogression systems that reward players for completing tasks. When a game is taken offline, certain milestonesand the trophies tied to thembecome permanently inaccessible. Its like buying a book and having the final chapters removed, or losing the ending of a film you already paid for. The experience becomes fundamentally incomplete. Under the objectives of the DFAparticularly regarding fair contract exit, transparency, and protection against manipulative or unfair designI respectfully urge the Commission to: - Provide rights or fallback protections when publishers retire games that consumers bought and still play; - Allow or require publishers to issue offline patches or peer-to-peer versions so communities can continue using games without corporate servers; - Treat removal of access to paid digital content as a consumer-rights issue, not just a technical or platform matter. Consumers deserve legal safeguards when the digital goods they purchasedincluding progression systems or achievementsare effectively erased. This aligns with the DFAs broader ambition to simplify and harmonise protection across digital content, combat unfair practices, and close gaps where current consumer law is silent. Please ensure that players arent left without recourse when games are shut offespecially those with active communities or ongoing achievements. We pay for experiences, not disappearing libraries or incomplete journeys. Thank you so much for taking the time to read my feedback. I deeply respect the effort being made to make digital spaces fairer for everyone.
